(880 Part II, F.2)
Has temporary or permanent seeding been installed, such that vehicle access may compromise the stabilization and potentially increase erosion?
Is yes, are/were inspections conducted on the same frequency as other activities?
Identify below, each location (0.25 miles above and below each access point) and observations at each location
Site construction conforms with approved Erosion & Sedimentation Control (ESC) plan
A properly implemented ESC plan should minimize erosion potential through the following actions:
All perimeter control practices such as silt fence, identified on the plan installed as a first step measure?
Soil stockpile and borrow areas properly stabilized and/or trapping measures installed?
Earthen structures (such as dams, dikes, diversions) stabilized immediately?
Cut and fill slopes are constructed in a manner to minimize erosion?
Sediment basins, traps, and barriers installed according to approved plan?
Concentrated runoff conveyed down a cut or fill slope in an adequate temporary or permanent channel, flume or slope drain structure?
Storm inlets made operable during construction are protected so sediment lade water cannot enter without first being filtered?
